message.content:
Create the file C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\token-budget-controller.md with this exact content:

```markdown
---
name: token-budget-controller
description: >
  Tracks and controls token burn rate across sessions. Use whenever starting a large task,
  after every session to log usage, or when token usage needs to be monitored.
  TRIGGERS: "check token usage", "how much budget left", "token budget",
  "optimize token usage", "/token-budget", "are we burning too fast".
  Also auto-triggers when session usage exceeds 50% of daily budget.
compatibility: [file-read, file-write]
---

# Token Budget Controller

Tracks rolling token usage per session. Warns at budget thresholds.
Adjusts agent parallelism dynamically to hit the 50-90% efficiency sweet spot.

## Daily Budget Context

- Daily limit: 15,000 tokens per 5-hour window
- Target usage: 50-90% (7,500–13,500 tokens per session)
- Minimum viable session: 20% (3,000 tokens) — below this, task is too small to justify overhead
- Maximum burn rate: 90%+ risks running out mid-task

## How It Works

Token usage comes from MiniMax agent notifications (`total_tokens` in task output).
Log each agent's tokens as notifications arrive. Track rolling total.

## Token Log File

Location: C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\_token_log.md

Format:
```
## Session: YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM

| Agent | Tokens | Duration (ms) |
| [agent-name] | [count] | [ms] |

Total session tokens: [sum]
Session duration: [sum ms]
```

## Thresholds and Actions

| Usage | Level | Action |
|-------|-------|--------|
| 0-20% | Idle | Spawn more agents, take on bigger tasks |
| 20-50% | Light | Normal dispatch, watch burn rate |
| 50-75% | Optimal | Warn user, suggest ending soon |
| 75-90% | Heavy | Suggest wrapping up non-critical agents |
| 90%+ | Critical | Stop spawning new agents, finish current ones |

## Burn Rate Calculation

Burn rate = rolling_total_tokens / session_elapsed_seconds
Tokens per minute = burn_rate * 60

Estimate time to empty:
```
remaining_budget = 15000 - rolling_total
time_remaining = remaining_budget / burn_rate_seconds
```

## Adaptive Parallelism

Adjust agent count based on remaining budget and burn rate:

- Budget >75%, burn_rate >200 tokens/sec: reduce to 1-2 agents
- Budget 50-75%, normal burn: 3-4 agents
- Budget <50%, low burn: 5+ agents (take on more work)

## Session Start

At session start:
1. Read _token_log.md for recent usage patterns
2. Check if today's already has logged sessions
3. Warn if previous sessions used >50% of budget
4. Calculate recommended agent parallelism for this session

## Session End

After every task:
1. Log all agent token counts and durations to _token_log.md
2. Calculate total session tokens
3. Calculate tokens per minute burn rate
4. Compare to target (50-90%)
5. Record whether session was under/over target
6. Append learnings to _learnings.md about token efficiency

## Warnings Format

When crossing thresholds, report to user:
"[THRESHOLD WARNING] Used X/Y tokens (Z%). Burn rate: N tokens/min. Est. Y minutes remaining at current rate. [recommendation]"
```

Create the _token_log.md file too at C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\_token_log.md with a template:

```markdown
# Token Usage Log

Daily budget: 15,000 tokens / 5 hours
Target: 50-90% per session

<!-- Sessions logged below -->

## Session: 2026-04-12

| Agent | Tokens | Duration (ms) |
|-------|-------|---------------|

Total: 0 tokens | 0ms
```

Do NOT write any Python scripts or executables. Only write these two markdown files.

content.thinking:
The user wants me to create two markdown files:

2. `C:\Users\Charlie\.claude\skills\_token_log.md` with a template

Let me create these files.
